signals.reasoning: Large-cap benchmark is stable with low squeeze risk and no strong mechanical edge, so the red macro keeps this in watch mode rather than a fresh entry.
ETH is flat with neutral positioning and only a weak funding shift, which is not enough to justify a trade while the broader regime stays risk-constrained.
SOL has a modest bullish mechanical bias and increasingly negative predicted funding, giving it squeeze-supportive structure, but macro red keeps conviction reduced.
The token is already up roughly 29% with positive funding, very wide spread, and poor liquidity quality, which makes it look more like a crowded chase than a clean long.
TAO is in a clear short-term down move with positive funding and a bearish mechanical bias, so it remains one of the cleaner downside names on the sheet.
MINA still screens as a squeeze-style long because funding is deeply negative and the mechanical board is strongly bullish, though the thin liquidity keeps size and confidence capped.
KAITO remains one of the clearest shorts because price is still breaking lower, funding is positive, and the board keeps it in bearish territory without any sign of squeeze support.
DASH has strong recent price momentum, but the move already looks stretched and the spread/liquidity quality is not good enough to chase or fade aggressively here.
PYTH keeps a constructive squeeze profile with negative funding, bullish mechanical bias, and acceptable tradability, making it a viable secondary long behind TNSR.
TNSR is still the best long on the board because funding remains strongly negative, squeeze risk is high, and the mechanical model keeps it in full trade territory despite the red macro backdrop.
VVV has decent trend strength, but the edge is muddied by positive funding and a neutral board, so it stays on watch rather than earning a fresh directional call.
